title: The Evolution of the LLM Toolstack - Thoughts on Capturing Value in the GenAI Tech Stack as a Startup
why_strong: Clear stack-stage taxonomy, VC strategic framing, concise sections that map to market stages and startup playbooks. Good model for market + wedge-oriented narrative.
weaknesses: VC perspective (bias toward investment outcomes); not an evidence-dense research report; few primary-source citations.

title: openai/evals (GitHub)
why_strong: Authoritative primary-source for evaluation frameworks; concrete repo structure and docs show how to run evals; good model for technical appendix and methodology sections.
weaknesses: Not a market report or narrative; focused narrowly on eval tooling rather than whole-market taxonomy.

title: Langfuse blog and OSS project (LLM observability)
why_strong: Product-centric, concrete examples of observability and tracing for LLMs; open-source project with technical details and deployment notes — useful exemplar for provider case studies and product analysis.
weaknesses: Vendor blog / product narrative may be promotional; not a neutral market analysis.

title: LangChain (GitHub)
why_strong: De-facto agent engineering framework and integration hub; strong traction and ecosystem; useful exemplar for developer experience and agent orchestration analyses.
weaknesses: Highly influential OSS project but also a commercial ecosystem (LangSmith) which must be accounted for when analyzing commercial dynamics.
